title: From The Whole To A Part
study: A Study In The Life Of John The
scriptureForWeek: VARIOUS PASSAGES IN THE GOSPELS Make sure you focus on the verses for each day, as these important events are taken from several readings of scripture across several chapters. These are chosen to highlight the ministry of John the Baptist and to frame up the gap between the Old and New Testament timelines.
weekTheme: Boys are Confused, Men have Clarity God will prepare you THE STORY IS BIGGER, WIDER, AND LONGER THAN YOU THINK. YOU HAVE AN IMPORTANT PART IN IT. One of the most amazing moments that can happen in a man’s life is when he figures out his calling. When he begins to discover who he is and why God has made him. It's amazing to know that there is a role in this life specifically designed for him. It can be hard to get to this point. There are so many options and voices crying out for your attention, so many people telling you how to live and what do to. It can be overwhelming. In this week’s sessions, we are going to see that God has a way of calling a man and filling his heart with fire. We are going to see this from the life of John the Baptist. John knew he had a miracle birth and that God was preparing him for something great, but it's going to come into focus for him. God is going to give him promises in his word that help him make sense of his childhood and how to take his place in the story of God. God has a unique calling for you. He chose you before the foundation of the world, determined the exact time and place that you should live, and has good works prepared in advance for you to walk in. He will begin to reveal what your role is, how to live it out, and give you courage to stand out in a fearful world. This week will help you wrestle and discern what God may have for you. Lean in. You never know what God may speak into your heart.

days.title: True Greatness
days.scripture: LUKE 1:13-17 But the angel said, “Don’t be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayer. Your wife, Elizabeth, will give you a son, and you are to name him John. You will have great joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth, for he will be great in the eyes of the Lord. He must never touch wine or other alcoholic drinks. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even before his birth. And he will turn many Israelites to the Lord their God. He will be a man with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will prepare the people for the coming of the Lord. He will turn the hearts of the fathers to their children, and he will cause those who are rebellious to accept the wisdom of the godly.”
days.reflection: Deep in the heart of every man is a desire for greatness. You probably sense it in your heart. Dallas Willard writes, "We were built to count, as water is made to run downhill. We are placed in a specific context to count in ways no one else does. That is our destiny.” When you dream about your future, you probably dream about doing something cool with your life. Something you are good at, that really matters. No one grows up thinking, “I want to be mediocre. I want to be a failure when I get older.” The desire to do something that matters, to be rewarded and recognized is something given to you by God. But you have to make sure that you understand what God means by greatness. Greatness is really distorted in the world today. Greatness in our culture means being rich or being famous or being the center of attention. It’s defined by winning, crushing opponents, and having power. That’s why it’s important that we get a vision of “being great in the sight of God.” God doesn’t get rid of the desire for greatness; he redefines and redirects it. He redefines it. He says greatness is about sacrifice. It's about how much you give, not how much you get. He says greatness is about your ability to think of others, not yourself. He says greatness is about the ability to serve, not show off. And God redirects our ambition for greatness. He says greatness is about what you do for others, not what you achieve for yourself. He says greatness is about how much you bless others, not if you beat them. He says greatness is about others flourishing, not your fame. Jesus called John the Baptist the greatest man ever born. Why? Was it because he was so good at speaking, or so good-looking, or so popular? No, it was because he was so committed to God’s purpose for his people, and because he was so willing to get out of the way to create space for Jesus. He was great because of his loyalty, sacrifice, and love. Martin Luther King. Jr. said, “everyone has the power for greatness, not for fame but greatness, because greatness is determined by service.” You may never be great in the eyes of the world. Only a tiny, tiny percentage of men ever will be. But you can be great in the eyes of God, and that’s what truly matters. To take your place in the story of God, get a vision to be great in the eyes of God. That’s where the joy is, the legacy is, and the fulfillment is. Seek Biblical greatness like John.
questions: Have you ever had a dream of doing something great? If so, would you be willing to share it?
Jesus is the greatest man who has ever lived. Can you list out a few things that make him so great? How is this different from greatness in the eyes of the world?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share what they think the greatest thing they have ever done is. Why?

thoughts.verse: Then they began to argue among themselves about who would be the greatest among them. Jesus told them, “In this world the kings and great men lord it over their people, yet they are called ‘friends of the people.’ But among you it will be different. Those who are the greatest among you should take the lowest rank, and the leader should be like a servant.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to stir a vision in you to be great in the eyes of God. Ask him to push away any apathy in your heart and cultivate some holy ambition.

days.title: The Men God Needs
days.scripture: LUKE 1:13-17 But the angel said, “Don’t be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayer. Your wife, Elizabeth, will give you a son, and you are to name him John. You will have great joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth, for he will be great in the eyes of the Lord. He must never touch wine or other alcoholic drinks. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even before his birth. And he will turn many Israelites to the Lord their God. He will be a man with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will prepare the people for the coming of the Lord. He will turn the hearts of the fathers to their children, and he will cause those who are rebellious to accept the wisdom of the godly.”
days.reflection: What’s the first thing that comes into your mind when you hear the word “prophet?” Maybe it’s a weird guy holding a sign saying “the end is near.” Maybe it’s someone whose eyes roll back into their head in a trance, who claims to have fantastic visions about the future. Our world today tends to think of prophets as people who claim to have special insights about the end times or those who can predict the future. In the Bible, God gave some prophets the ability to know future events, but that wasn’t their main job. A prophet was designed to tell people what God really thought about how they were living in the moment. The prophet called people back to faithfulness to God and to live according to his will. Prophets called people to turn away from idols, turn away from treating people poorly, and to focus on their relationship with God. A prophet was someone who turned the hearts of people back to God. John the Baptist was a prophet sent from God. It says he was sent in the “spirit and power of Elijah.” Elijah was an incredible prophet in the Old Testament. He lived at a time when Israel was under terrible, godless leadership. Ahab and Jezebel were in charge and they rebelled against God. They worshipped Baal and led the nation astray. Elijah showed up to confront them for their godless leadership. One of his most famous moments is a showdown with the prophets of Baal. He calls down fire from heaven, kills the prophets of Baal, and calls the people to choose the one true God. John was sent to play a similar role. He was going to call God's people back to faithfulness to him. He was going to tell them to be loyal to God, turn away injustice, and make space for a new move of God. John was a fresh voice with a timeless message: “get ready for God.” And, man, was he effective. John stirred up the hearts of the people and won their attention. He pointed people back to God and got their hearts ready. In every generation, God is looking for young men with a prophetic spirit. Young men, full of courage, willing to confront what is wrong with the world. Young men willing to remind people how they are called to live. Young men willing to stand up for what is right. Believe it or not, God wants to use you in your generation to be a man like that. A man of conviction who is not controlled by what others think. A man of courage willing to stand alone. A man willing to call people back to God. And you have something better than the spirit of Elijah. You have the Holy Spirit. Satan wants you to be full of fear. To be a slave to what other people think about you. God wants you to be bold. To be filled with faith and fire and to wake the world up from its sin. It's hard to go against the grain and to stand up on your own. But it's also amazing. There are incredible rewards for the righteous. And even if people don’t like you, they will respect you. God wants to raise you up. You were born for such a time as this. Lean in.
questions: Have you ever thought of yourself as someone God could use to influence others? Why or why not?
Who is one of the most courageous people you know? What about them makes them so brave?
If you could get every one of your friends to learn one thing about God, what would it be? Why?

thoughts.verse: "But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon you. And you will be my witnesses, telling people about me everywhere—in Jerusalem, throughout Judea, in Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.”
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you courage to share about him with your friends around you. Ask him for the power of the Holy Spirit mentioned in the verse above.

days.title: The Heart Of The Matter
days.scripture: LUKE 1:13-17 But the angel said, “Don’t be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayer. Your wife, Elizabeth, will give you a son, and you are to name him John. You will have great joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth, for he will be great in the eyes of the Lord. He must never touch wine or other alcoholic drinks. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even before his birth. And he will turn many Israelites to the Lord their God. He will be a man with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will prepare the people for the coming of the Lord. He will turn the hearts of the fathers to their children, and he will cause those who are rebellious to accept the wisdom of the godly.”
days.reflection: What do you think God wants from you? Do you think he wants you to be good? To be nice? To do the right thing? These are all secondary issues to God. What he wants from you the most is your heart. Lots of guys your age don’t understand this about God. They think he is a moral policeman out to get them if they screw up. Most people think that what God cares about the most is making bad people good. They think that he came to give us a set of rules to follow and he exists to punish us when we fail to live up to them. But what God wants the most, more than changing our behavior or getting us to live a different way, is our hearts. God is after your heart. Our hearts are the most important part about us. That’s why one of the most important scriptures you can know says: “Guard your heart above all else, for it determines the course of your life" (Proverbs 4:23). If you are going to take your place in the story of God, you have to align your heart with his. But our hearts can be tricky things. Jeremiah 17:9-10 says. “The human heart is the most deceitful of all things, and desperately wicked. Who really knows how bad it is? But I, the LORD, search all hearts and examine secret motives.” When God gets ready to move, he raises up men to speak to people’s hearts. This was what God did with John the Baptist. God used him to turn many of his people back to the Lord their God. He reminded them of God's work, eased their fears, and confronted their challenges. He helped hopeless and discouraged people see that God was on the move again. If you are going to take your place in the story of God, you need to realize that God wants to turn your heart toward him and use you to turn the hearts of others to him. You may be worried that following Jesus will make you into a goody two shoes. You may be worried that Christians walk around condemning others and thinking they are better than everyone else. But that’s not what it means to live for God. God wants to use you to care for people’s hearts. To turn them from death to life, discouragement to hope, distraction to purpose, darkness to light. God isn’t a moral policeman; he is a heart surgeon. He comes to make that which is sick healthy so that we can live with freedom and joy. God used John the Baptist to turn the hearts of many back to God. He wants to use you to do the same thing in your generation. Turn your heart to him now!
questions: Can you name a couple of things that you see people your age wanting with all their hearts? What do they seem to crave or ache for?
Why do you think God starts his work by focusing on our hearts and not our behavior? Why do you think this order might be important?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they learned to guard their heart. What have they had to guard against, and what have they had to cultivate?

thoughts.verse: That is why the LORD says, “Turn to me now, while there is time. Give me your hearts. Come with fasting, weeping, and mourning. Don’t tear your clothing in your grief, but tear your hearts instead.” Return to the LORD your God, for he is merciful and compassionate, slow to get angry and filled with unfailing love.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to turn your heart toward him. If there is any sin or distractions in your heart turning you away from him, ask for forgiveness and for him to give you an undivided heart.

days.title: Fathers And Sons
days.scripture: MALACHI 4:5-6 AND LUKE 1:13-17 “Look, I am sending you the prophet Elijah before the great and dreadful day of the LORD arrives. His preaching will turn the hearts of fathers to their children, and the hearts of children to their fathers. Otherwise I will come and strike the land with a curse.” But the angel said, “Don’t be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayer. Your wife, Elizabeth, will give you a son, and you are to name him John. You will have great joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth, for he will be great in the eyes of the Lord. He must never touch wine or other alcoholic drinks. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even before his birth. And he will turn many Israelites to the Lord their God. He will be a man with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will prepare the people for the coming of the Lord. He will turn the hearts of the fathers to their children, and he will cause those who are rebellious to accept the wisdom of the godly.”
days.reflection: Did you know that you are being raised in the middle of one of the greatest crises of history? It’s not a food crisis, an environment crisis, or an economic crisis (as bad as these might be). You are being raised in the middle of a fatherhood crisis. Did you know that when a child is raised in a father-absent home, they are: At 4x greater risk of poverty More likely to have behavioral problems More likely to go to prison More likely to commit crime 7x more likely to become pregnant as a teen More likely to face abuse and neglect More likely to abuse drugs and alcohol 2x more likely to be obese 2x more likely to drop out of school 90% of runaway and homeless children are from fatherless homes 63% of youth suicides happen in households with an absent father Yet, no one seems to talk about this. We just accept broken homes and families as normal. Yet God cares a TON about relationships between fathers and their kids. Fathers play a massive role in shaping a young man’s view of themselves, their faith, and their sense of worth. That’s why God cares about healing generational brokenness. God closed the Old Testament with a vision of turning the hearts of fathers and sons to each other, and part of the role of John the Baptist was to turn the hearts of fathers and sons to each other. When a son has a good relationship with his father, he will: Have more confidence. Do better in the workforce. Have a healthy understanding of sex, relationships, and marriage. Do better in school. Navigate challenges. Have more resilience to face life’s challenges. Prophets repair what has been broken by sin. God uses men to turn men to each other. That’s why doing the Primal Path is so important. You and your Dad are restoring what has been broken. Though it may not seem like much, you are helping repair what is wrong with the world. This doesn’t mean that you are going to have a perfect relationship with your dad, but you are working to have a good one. You have no idea in the days to come how much of an advantage this will be and the kind of foundation you will have to build on. And you and your dad can inspire others. They can look at your relationship as an example of men getting it right. Men with honor, men with integrity, men with courage. Keep going, be grateful, and keep growing with your dad. You are going to need it more than you ever know growing up. Turn to each other, and never turn away.
questions: Take a moment to ask your dad about his relationship with his Dad.What was good about it? Where was there pain?
Ask your dad what he learned from his dad, and how it has helped him in his life.
Is there anything you want to focus on, work on, or improve in your relationship with your dad? Be honest, and share it with him.

thoughts.verse: My child, never forget the things I have taught you. Store my commands in your heart. If you do this, you will live many years, and your life will be satisfying.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you from it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank God for your dad. Ask God to protect your relationship and to help you be honest and vulnerable with each other.

days.title: Reaching The Rebellious
days.scripture: LUKE 1:57-79 But the angel said, “Don’t be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayer. Your wife, Elizabeth, will give you a son, and you are to name him John. You will have great joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth, for he will be great in the eyes of the Lord. He must never touch wine or other alcoholic drinks. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even before his birth. And he will turn many Israelites to the Lord their God. He will be a man with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will prepare the people for the coming of the Lord. He will turn the hearts of the fathers to their children, and he will cause those who are rebellious to accept the wisdom of the godly.”
days.reflection: The book of Proverbs talks about 2 kinds of people. The wise and the foolish. You are living in an age of fools. You are in the process of becoming wise. Wise men learn who God is. They learn what he wants. They learn how the world works. Fools rebel and live by their own wisdom. Much of the chaos in our world today exists because people have rebelled against God's design for the world. In Romans 1, Paul shows what happens when people rebel against God. He says that rebels: Think up foolish ideas about what God is like. Their minds become dark and their thinking gets confused. They worship stupid stuff and swap the creator for creation. They trade truth for lies. They trade love for hate. This brings rebellious people under judgment. Romans 1:28-32 says: Since they thought it foolish to acknowledge God, he abandoned them to their foolish thinking and let them do things that should never be done. Their lives became full of every kind of wickedness, sin, greed, hate, envy, murder, quarreling, deception, malicious behavior, and gossip. They are backstabbers, haters of God, insolent, proud, and boastful. They invent new ways of sinning, and they disobey their parents. They refuse to understand, break their promises, are heartless, and have no mercy. They know God’s justice requires that those who do these things deserve to die, yet they do them anyway. Worse yet, they encourage others to do them, too. The crazy thing is that though this was written 2000 years ago, it sounds like the evening news. Stories like this fill the media and are everywhere in our culture. Rebellious people cause harm. Fools are filled with heartache. Because God loves people and wants what’s best for them, he will raise up messengers to turn the rebels back to God and help fools become wise. This happens when they see the error of their ways and the goodness of God. John the Baptist helped people turn back to God. He was able to show the fools how to become wise and the rebels to become loyal. This is a key part of what God wants to do in your life. He wants to make you a wise man who can instruct others, A man who knows how to be both loyal and loving, confrontive yet kind, wise and winsome. This week, you have seen how God gives each man a role and an invitation in their generation. He made John the Baptist great in the sight of God, a prophet to speak God's word, a man to turn people’s hearts back to God, to restore fathers and sons, and to make the rebellious wise. God seeks to do this in your generation, and he wants to use someone just like you. So surrender yourself to him, don’t get freaked out, and keep preparing. You are on the right track and at the right pace. Keep moving forward; there are some glory days ahead.
questions: What are some of the ways guys your age tend to rebel against God? How do you see this hurting them?
What are some of the ways you feel the culture telling you to rebel against God?
What do you think some of the rewards of being wise are? How can you pursue becoming wise?

thoughts.verse: LORD, you are mine! I promise to obey your words! With all my heart I want your blessings. Be merciful as you promised. I pondered the direction of my life, and I turned to follow your laws. I will hurry, without delay, to obey your commands. Evil people try to drag me into sin, but I am firmly anchored to your instructions.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a heart of wisdom, Ask him to use you to speak to any of your friends getting caught up in rebellion with the right words to help them.

extras: WEEK 3 MANSCHOOL TRUE GREATNESS MANSCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: FAMILY TREE In our world today, greatness is thought of as public success. Adoring fans and tons of applause. But for Jesus, greatness is about servanthood, and many times it goes unnoticed by the world. This week, to learn to grow in biblical greatness, consider creating a care package for someone you love, but do it anonymously. Find someone you think would really value some encouragement. Go shopping for things you think they may love, assemble them together, and then send it off in the mail. Write them a kind and encouraging note, but do your best to stay anonymous. Then enjoy the beauty of knowing you have served, loved, and blessed someone, and have taken steps to grow in biblical greatness. OPTION 2. WATCH COACH CARTER The power of one man to live prophetically and turn the hearts of a team to each other and out of rebellion into wisdom. You need to determine how age-appropriate this is for your son. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aA1Npd9d9cM OPTION 3. DINNER AND DISCUSSION Take your son out to one of his favorite restaurants or for deserts and talk through: The difference between being great in the eyes of God verses the eyes of the world. What it means to be “prophetic” in how we speak to others.
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
